message:
feat(abi): add robust mutex ownership, SA_SIGINFO handler dispatch, mkdtemp/posix_openpt, and fix sysconf _SC_NPROCESSORS_ONLN

pthread: PTHREAD_MUTEX_ROBUST attribute with owner TID tracking, EOWNERDEAD
on dead-owner lock, ENOTRECOVERABLE after failed recovery.

signal: SA_SIGINFO three-argument dispatch with siginfo_t/ucontext_t. New
test verifies si_signo and si_code fields.

stdlib: mkdtemp (unique temp directory) and posix_openpt (/dev/ptmx master).

unistd: sysconf(_SC_NPROCESSORS_ONLN) parses /proc/stat instead of returning 1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-am9u","title":"EPIC: Dynamic System Constants — Replace hard-coded values with runtime queries","description":"FrankenLibC currently hard-codes several system-dependent constants (page size, stack limits, group counts, FD limits) that should be queried at runtime for cross-architecture correctness.

BACKGROUND: While FrankenLibC currently targets x86_64 Linux, these hard-coded values create latent bugs that will surface when porting to ARM64 (16KB/64KB pages), RISC-V, or systems with non-standard configurations. Even on x86_64, some values (like supplementary group count) can differ from defaults.

SCOPE: This epic covers all hard-coded constants identified in the comprehensive audit across malloc_abi.rs, unistd_abi.rs, glibc_internal_abi.rs, stdlib_abi.rs, pthread_abi.rs, and process_abi.rs.

ALREADY DONE: sysconf(_SC_PAGESIZE) now queries AT_PAGESZ from /proc/self/auxv at runtime (cached). The mmap_alloc bootstrap path intentionally keeps 4096 as the minimum alignment (documented).

RATIONALE: AGENTS.md says "We want to do things the RIGHT way with NO TECH DEBT" — hard-coded system constants are textbook tech debt.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:22.060285167Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:22.060285167Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-am9u.1","title":"Replace hard-coded 64-group limit with dynamic NGROUPS_MAX query","description":"FILE: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/glibc_internal_abi.rs:4592
CURRENT: let mut groups = [0u32; 64]
PROBLEM: NGROUPS_MAX can be 65536 on modern Linux. Hard-coding 64 silently truncates group membership for users in >64 groups, breaking permission checks.
FIX: Use a two-pass approach — first call getgroups(0, NULL) to get count, then allocate dynamically. Or use sysconf(_SC_NGROUPS_MAX) to size the buffer.
IMPACT: Any program calling getgroups() or initgroups() for users in many groups will get truncated results.
CONSIDERATION: The Vec allocation here is fine since this isn't a bootstrap-critical path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:51.199909661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:46.904224615Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:46.903226145Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-am9u.1","depends_on_id":"bd-am9u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:51.199909661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-am9u.2","title":"Replace hard-coded FD_SETSIZE=1024 with libc::FD_SETSIZE","description":"FILES: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs:2283-2287, glibc_internal_abi.rs:3074, fortify_abi.rs:1001
CURRENT: Hard-coded bounds check !(0..1024) and [u8; 128] arrays (128 = 1024/8).
PROBLEM: While glibc itself uses FD_SETSIZE=1024, using the constant name is better than magic numbers. More importantly, the fd_set TLS buffer in rpc_abi.rs is sized to exactly 128 bytes — should derive from libc::FD_SETSIZE / 8.
FIX: Replace magic number 1024 with libc::FD_SETSIZE constant. Replace 128-byte arrays with [u8; libc::FD_SETSIZE as usize / 8].
IMPACT: Medium — affects programs using select() with fd > 1024, though poll/epoll is preferred.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:52.321359098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:39:46.006562978Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:39:46.004805211Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-am9u.2","depends_on_id":"bd-am9u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:52.321359098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-am9u.3","title":"Increase CPU affinity mask from 128 bytes (1024 CPUs) to support larger systems","description":"FILE: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:1804
CURRENT: let mut mask = [0u8; 128] — supports max 1024 CPUs.
PROBLEM: Large servers can have >1024 logical CPUs (e.g., AMD EPYC with 256 cores + SMT = 512, multi-socket = 1024+). The mask should be sized to the actual system or use a reasonable upper bound.
FIX: Either use a larger static buffer (e.g., 512 bytes = 4096 CPUs) or dynamically query the kernel for the actual affinity mask size. The sched_getaffinity syscall returns the actual size used, so we could do a two-pass approach.
IMPACT: Low — only affects very large servers. Current 1024 CPU limit is sufficient for most systems.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:53.388076511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:53.388076511Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-am9u.3","depends_on_id":"bd-am9u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:53.388076511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-am9u.4","title":"Make default thread stack size configurable via environment variable","description":"FILES: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:2488,2494
CURRENT: ATTR_DEFAULT_STACK_SIZE = 2 * 1024 * 1024 (2 MiB), ATTR_MIN_STACK_SIZE = 16384 (16 KiB)
CONTEXT: glibc uses a default of 8 MiB, musl uses 128 KiB. Our 2 MiB is a reasonable middle ground but differs from both.
FIX: Keep 2 MiB as default but allow override via FRANKENLIBC_THREAD_STACK_SIZE env var. The minimum should match PTHREAD_STACK_MIN from the system.
IMPACT: Low — 2 MiB works for most applications. Some embedded or memory-constrained scenarios might want smaller stacks.
CONSIDERATION: Don't read env var on every thread creation — cache it at init time.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:54.503610731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:54.503610731Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-am9u.4","depends_on_id":"bd-am9u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:54.503610731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-b5a","title":"Deterministic E2E suite v2: smoke/stress/fault/stability with replay","description":"Background:
- Passing unit/conformance tests is necessary but insufficient; final confidence requires deterministic end-to-end behavior under realistic programs and fault scenarios.

Goal:
- Build v2 deterministic e2e suite covering smoke, stress, fault-injection, and long-run stability in strict and hardened modes.

Deliverables:
1) Scenario catalog with required strict/hardened execution pairs.
2) Deterministic replay controls (fixed seeds, pinned env, scenario manifests).
3) Artifact capture for every run (logs, diffs, summaries, crash bundles where applicable).
4) CI integration with clear failure triage outputs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Clean checkout executes all e2e scenarios reproducibly.
- Failures are diagnosable without rerunning interactively.
- E2E gates become a release blocker for target streams.

Test and Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for scenario manifest parsing and replay controls.
- E2E scripts per scenario class with strict+hardened assertions.
- Structured logs: trace_id, scenario_id, mode, subsystem, outcome, errno, timing, artifact_refs.

Extreme Optimization Requirements:
- Include perf-sensitive e2e scenarios to catch regressions that unit microbenches miss.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:34.530526947Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:59:54.952280112Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:59:54.952202968Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e","testing","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1068,"issue_id":"bd-b5a","author":"RedLotus","text":"Closed by RedLotus after completion of child tasks bd-b5a.1, bd-b5a.2, and bd-b5a.3.  {"id":"bd-reax","title":"EPIC: Replace critical stubs with real implementations or host delegation","description":"The comprehensive audit identified several categories of stub/placeholder implementations:

1. SAFE NO-OP STUBS (~50 functions): Return 0/-1/NULL without doing real work. These are intentional for features we don't support (profiling, NSS, netgroups). LOW priority — they don't crash, just silently no-op.

2. HOST CALL-THROUGH DEBT (~70 symbols): Functions that delegate to host glibc via RTLD_NEXT. These work correctly but represent symbols we haven't implemented natively yet. LOW priority — they work fine via delegation.

3. CORE TERMIOS STUBS: tcgetattr/tcsetattr/tcflush/tcflow in frankenlibc-core return -1. NOT a real issue because the ABI layer (termios_abi.rs) uses raw ioctl syscalls directly and never calls these core stubs.

4. RPC SUBSYSTEM (~68 stubs): Return safe defaults. MEDIUM priority — RPC is legacy but some programs still use it.

5. ELF LOADER INCOMPLETE: Only Phase-1 relocations; IFUNC deferred. MEDIUM priority — affects shared library loading.

RATIONALE: Focus on items that break real programs first. The setjmp/longjmp stubs were already fixed (now delegate to host). The on_exit handler gap was already fixed.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:55.983549992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:55.983549992Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-reax.1","title":"Add host delegation for critical RPC functions (xdr_*, clnt_*)","description":"FILES: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs
CURRENT: ~68 RPC symbols use rpc_native! macro returning safe defaults (0/NULL).
PROBLEM: Programs using Sun RPC (portmap, NFS tools, ypbind) get silent failures.
FIX: For the most commonly used RPC functions (clntudp_create, clnttcp_create, xdr_int, xdr_string, etc.), resolve the host glibc symbol via our ELF resolver and delegate. Keep the safe-default fallback for symbols not found in host.
CONSIDERATION: RPC is deprecated and being replaced by gRPC. Many modern systems don't even have RPC tools installed. This is low priority unless a specific program needs it.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:56.142778940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:56.142778940Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-reax.1","depends_on_id":"bd-reax","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:56.142778940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-reax.2","title":"Add IFUNC relocation support to ELF loader","description":"FILES: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/elf/loader.rs:222
CURRENT: Only Phase-1 relocations supported. IFUNC (STT_GNU_IFUNC) relocations are deferred.
PROBLEM: glibc itself uses IFUNC for CPU-specific optimized versions of memcpy, memset, etc. When we try to resolve these from the host ELF, we may get the IFUNC resolver instead of the final address.
FIX: Detect IFUNC symbols and call the resolver function to get the actual implementation address.
IMPACT: Affects host_resolve.rs which parses the glibc ELF to find symbols like malloc, dlvsym, etc. If any of these are IFUNCs, we'd get the wrong address.
CONSIDERATION: On modern x86_64 glibc, malloc and dlvsym are NOT IFUNCs (they're direct symbols), but memcpy/memset ARE IFUNCs. Since we only resolve non-IFUNC symbols from host_resolve.rs, this may not be an immediate problem.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:57.285183747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:57.285183747Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-reax.2","depends_on_id":"bd-reax","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:57.285183747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- TLS handoff correctness is required for thread-local errno and future pthread_key destructor behavior.

Goal:
- Implement and verify TLS initialization/teardown handoff for spawned threads.

Deliverables:
1) TLS init protocol during thread start.
2) Per-thread errno/TLS storage linkage checks.
3) Teardown sequencing and cleanup invariants.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Thread-local values remain isolated and stable under concurrent workloads.
- TLS teardown leaves no leaked state.

- Unit tests for TLS setup/teardown and isolation.
- E2E concurrent tests validating cross-thread isolation.

 {"id":"bd-ule","title":"EPIC: New runtime_math kernels (FDR, SOS, localization, Groebner, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy)","description":"Goal: add new alien-artifact kernels that compile down to tiny deterministic guards/dispatch, without blowing hot-path budgets.

- Each kernel must specify hot-path cost, update cadence, cached state surface, and a test strategy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.520777833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54.998226285Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:38.823974086Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-1v2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2vf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2wd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-3ld","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-45d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-abi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-cv9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-gn9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-l2r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1145,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: New runtime_math Kernels (How They Serve libc)

### Purpose
runtime_math is the control plane for the membrane: it decides when to stay fast vs go deep, and when to repair vs deny. New kernels are allowed only if they compile to **small deterministic runtime artifacts** (tables, tiny evaluators) with *explicit* overhead.

### Reverse Core Map Anchoring (Surface -> Failure Class -> Math -> Runtime Artifact)
These kernels must tie to concrete libc pressure points:
- **Hot string/memory kernels** -> overlap/alignment/dispatch faults -> localization + proof-carrying tables -> certified dispatch tables
- **Allocator** -> temporal/provenance corruption + contention regimes -> approachability + SOS barrier -> admissibility guard + multi-objective controller
- **Loader/symbol/IFUNC** -> compatibility drift -> Groebner normal forms + localization -> canonical “violation signatures” + resolver table witnesses
- **Conformance monitoring (many tests, many monitors)** -> multiple-testing false escalations -> alpha-investing FDR -> controller that caps false alarms across families
- **Harness probe selection** -> coverage gaps -> Sobol -> deterministic low-discrepancy probe schedules under budget

### Branch-Diversity Reminder (Project Rule)
Major milestones must include at least:
- conformal statistics: already in-tree (`conformal`, `eprocess`) + this epic adds FDR
- algebraic topology: already in-tree (`cohomology`, `persistence`)
- abstract algebra: this epic adds Groebner
- Grothendieck-Serre methods: already in-tree (`higher_topos`) + localization is in the same “geometry/localization” vein

### Runtime Cost Discipline (Non-Negotiable)
For each kernel we require:
- hot-path invocation budget (ns/op) and cadence (per call vs per epoch)
- cached state surface (what lives in `RuntimeKernelSnapshot`)
- deterministic arithmetic (fixed-point if possible; no heavy transcendentals)
- tests that show behavior invariants and that perf gates remain within budget

### Deliverable Shape
Each kernel follows the same pipeline:
1. Design bead: math contract + loss model + artifact format + invariants
2. Implement bead: tiny runtime evaluator/controller (no heavy machinery)
3. Integrate bead: wire into `RuntimeMathKernel::decide/observe` with versioned snapshot fields
4. Tests/perf bead: microbench + property tests + regression thresholds","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:51:50Z"},{"id":1146,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCompass: All 9 blockers now CLOSED.  {"id":"bd-vj3","title":"Perf: Per-kernel microbench suite (criterion groups + thresholds)","description":"Add a criterion microbench suite that measures *per-kernel* costs, not just end-to-end decide().

## Why
End-to-end `decide()` can hide regressions. Per-kernel benches let us pin blame when a new kernel or refactor shifts cost.

## Bench Design
- Criterion group per kernel (or per family of kernels).
- Measure:
  - `decide` contributions (hot modules)
  - cadence update costs (epoch modules)
- Report ns/op and p95/p99 where applicable.

## Dependency
- Must reuse the baseline harness from `bd-d5l` and regression thresholds from `bd-242`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new kernel cannot land without adding/adjusting its microbench.
- Thresholds catch >X% regressions (configurable) in CI.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightMoose","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:03:30.833286241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:48.417613055Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:39.911611946Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-242","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-d5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-pt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1147,"issue_id":"bd-vj3","author":"BrightMoose","text":"Added per-kernel microbench binary crates/glibc-rs-bench/benches/runtime_math_kernels_bench.rs (machine-readable RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines) and wired it in crates/glibc-rs-bench/Cargo.toml.
